name: EasySave
title: EasySave | Build a Disciplined Savings Habit
description: Learn how to enrol in EasySave through Nobles Vault or with staff support at the Nobles Cooperative office in Awka.
lede: A Nobles savings option for members who want practical support to build a consistent savings habit.
who: Members who want to begin or strengthen a personal savings routine with support from Nobles Cooperative.
problem: It can be difficult to separate money for future needs when household and business demands compete for the same cash.
works: Choose EasySave as the savings option you want to discuss.
Ask staff to explain the currently approved contribution and access rules.
Enrol through Nobles Vault or complete assisted enrolment where required.
Follow the agreed savings plan and review progress in the approved channel.
benefits: A named savings pathway inside the Nobles product range.
Access to human support through WhatsApp and the Awka office.
A structured next step for building savings discipline.
terms: Contribution amount and frequency: confirm the current approved terms with Nobles staff before enrolment.
Interest or earnings: no approved figure is available in the repository.
Withdrawal and early-access rules: confirm before making a contribution.
notice: Confirm the contribution, access and withdrawal conditions before enrolling. Choose an amount that leaves enough money for essential household and business needs.
faqs: Does EasySave have an approved interest rate?
No EasySave rate is approved in the current repository source. Ask Nobles staff for the current terms before enrolling.
How do I start?
Use Nobles Vault, contact the official WhatsApp support line, or visit the Awka office for assisted enrolment.
Can I withdraw at any time?
The repository does not contain an approved withdrawal rule. Confirm access conditions with staff before contributing.

name: Future Fund
title: Future Fund | Save Toward a Future Goal
description: Explore Future Fund, the Nobles Cooperative savings product that earns interest according to its approved tenor schedule.
lede: A goal-led savings product that earns interest according to the approved Future Fund tenor schedule.
who: Members planning toward a future financial goal who can keep money within an agreed tenor.
problem: Longer-term goals can be disrupted when savings remain mixed with money needed for everyday spending.
works: Define the future goal you want to fund.
Ask Nobles staff for the current approved tenor schedule and the terms attached to each tenor.
Select a suitable approved tenor before enrolment.
Enrol through Nobles Vault or with staff assistance and follow the agreed plan.
benefits: A goal-led plan rather than a monthly habit challenge.
Interest according to the approved tenor schedule.
Staff support when choosing an appropriate tenor.
terms: Interest: paid according to the approved Future Fund tenor schedule.
Tenors and corresponding rates: confirm the current approved schedule with staff; it is not present in the repository.
Contribution, maturity and early-access rules: confirm before enrolment.
notice: Future Fund is distinct from Monthly Savings Challenge. Confirm the tenor, maturity date and early-access consequences before committing money needed for a future goal.
faqs: Is Future Fund the same as Monthly Savings Challenge?
No. Future Fund is a tenor-based, interest-earning savings product. Monthly Savings Challenge is presented separately as a savings-discipline challenge.
What interest will I earn?
Interest follows the approved tenor schedule. The schedule is not included in the current repository, so staff must confirm it before enrolment.
Can I access the money before maturity?
No approved early-access rule is available in the repository. Confirm the condition for your selected tenor before enrolling.
approvalTodos: Approved tenor schedule and corresponding rates

name: Gold Vault
title: Gold Vault | 14.4% Per Annum for 12 Months
description: Gold Vault offers the approved current figure of 14.4% per annum for a 12-month term through Nobles Cooperative.
lede: A 12-month Nobles savings product with an approved return of 14.4% per annum.
who: Members considering a 12-month savings commitment who have first confirmed that the term suits their needs.
problem: Money intended for a longer-term goal may be spent early when it remains available for everyday use.
works: Review the 12-month commitment and responsible-use notice.
Ask staff to confirm the current contribution and maturity procedures.
Enrol through Nobles Vault or complete assisted enrolment where required.
Keep the funds for the approved 12-month term.
benefits: One approved term: 12 months.
One approved current figure: 14.4% per annum.
Access to Nobles staff support before enrolment.
terms: Approved return: 14.4% per annum.
Approved term: 12 months.
Minimum, maximum, payment method and early-access conditions: confirm with staff before enrolment.
notice: The 14.4% figure applies per annum to the approved 12-month term. Do not commit emergency money or funds needed for near-term household or business expenses. Confirm all access and maturity conditions first.
faqs: What is the approved Gold Vault figure?
The approved current figure is 14.4% per annum for a 12-month term.
Is the old website range still current?
No. The website uses the approved current figure of 14.4% per annum for 12 months.
What happens if I need the money early?
The repository does not contain an approved early-access rule. Confirm this with staff before enrolling.
approvalTodos: Early-access rules and any applicable charges

name: EduSave
title: EduSave | Plan Toward Education Costs
description: Learn how to discuss an education-focused savings goal through EduSave with Nobles Cooperative staff.
lede: A Nobles savings option for members who want to plan toward education-related costs.
who: Members preparing for education expenses and seeking a structured savings conversation with Nobles staff.
problem: Education expenses can create pressure when planning begins too close to the payment date.
works: Identify the education expense and the date by which it may be needed.
Ask staff to confirm the currently approved EduSave participation rules.
Agree an affordable plan through an approved enrolment channel.
Track progress and review the plan with staff when necessary.
benefits: Keeps an education goal visible.
Supports earlier planning instead of a last-minute response.
Provides access to Nobles Vault and physical staff assistance.
terms: Contribution, duration, beneficiary and payment rules: confirm with Nobles staff.
Interest or earnings: no approved figure is available in the repository.
Withdrawal and missed-contribution rules: confirm before enrolment.
notice: EduSave is a planning tool, not a guarantee that every education cost will be covered. Confirm the rules and choose contributions that remain affordable.
faqs: Does EduSave pay an approved interest rate?
No EduSave rate is available in the approved repository source. Staff must confirm current terms.
Can I use it for any school or learner?
Approved beneficiary and payment rules are not present in the repository. Confirm them before enrolment.
Where can I enrol?
Start through Nobles Vault or request assistance through the official WhatsApp line or Awka office.

name: Group Savings
title: Group Savings | Official Nobles Cooperative Product
description: Ask Nobles staff about the approved participation rules for Group Savings, the official product name.
lede: The official Nobles Group Savings product for members who want to explore saving with an eligible group.
who: Members considering a group-based savings arrangement after reviewing the approved participation and governance rules.
problem: Informal group arrangements can become unclear when participation, records and responsibilities are not agreed in advance.
works: Contact Nobles staff with information about the proposed group.
Review the currently approved eligibility, record-keeping and participation rules.
Complete the required group and member enrolment steps.
Use only the approved Nobles channel for contributions and records.
benefits: Uses the official product name Group Savings.
Provides a staff-assisted route for understanding participation rules.
Encourages clear records and agreed responsibilities before contributions begin.
terms: Group eligibility, size, leadership and contribution rules: confirm with staff.
Distribution, withdrawal and missed-contribution rules: no approved details are available in the repository.
Returns or interest: no approved figure is available in the repository.
notice: Do not contribute until every participant understands the approved rules, responsibilities and access conditions. Group Savings must not be described as an informal rotation scheme unless an approved source explicitly establishes that structure.
faqs: What is the official product name?
The official name is Group Savings.
Is Group Savings an informal rotation scheme?
The approved repository source does not establish that structure, so the website does not describe it that way.
How does a group enrol?
Contact Nobles staff for the current group eligibility and enrolment process.

name: Monthly Savings Challenge
title: Monthly Savings Challenge | Strengthen Savings Discipline
description: Explore the Monthly Savings Challenge as a distinct Nobles savings-discipline pathway and confirm current participation rules with staff.
lede: A savings-discipline challenge presented separately from the tenor-based Future Fund product.
who: Members who want a recurring monthly prompt to strengthen their savings habit.
problem: A savings goal can lose momentum when there is no regular rhythm for setting money aside.
works: Choose the savings goal you want the challenge to support.
Ask staff for the currently approved challenge duration and contribution rules.
Enrol through the approved Nobles channel.
Make contributions according to the confirmed challenge rules.
benefits: Provides a clear monthly savings focus.
Is explicitly separate from Future Fund and its tenor-based interest schedule.
Offers staff assistance through WhatsApp and the Awka office.
terms: Challenge duration and monthly contribution rules: confirm with staff.
Interest or rewards: no approved figure is available in the repository.
Missed-contribution and withdrawal rules: confirm before joining.
notice: Choose a monthly amount that is realistic after essential expenses. The challenge does not use the Future Fund tenor schedule unless an approved product source later states otherwise.
faqs: Is this the same as Future Fund?
No. Monthly Savings Challenge is a savings-discipline challenge. Future Fund is a separate tenor-based product that earns interest according to its approved schedule.
How much must I save monthly?
No approved minimum or maximum is available in the repository. Confirm the current rule with staff.
Does the challenge earn interest or a reward?
No approved interest or reward rule is available in the repository.

name: Food Vault
title: Food Vault | Confirm the Current Approved Plan
description: Contact Nobles Cooperative staff to confirm the current approved Food Vault participation and fulfilment rules before enrolling.
lede: A Nobles product whose detailed food-plan structure must be confirmed with staff before enrolment.
who: Members interested in planning ahead for food needs after reviewing the current approved Food Vault structure.
problem: Food planning can create household pressure when purchases and contributions are not prepared for in advance.
works: Contact Nobles staff and request the current approved Food Vault plan.
Review the confirmed contribution, product, timing and fulfilment rules.
Complete enrolment only through the approved channel.
Keep the confirmed plan details and contribution records.
benefits: Creates a dedicated conversation about planning for food needs.
Provides official staff support before any contribution.
Keeps unapproved seasonal or fulfilment claims off the public website.
terms: The repository does not confirm an approved Onwa Dezemba Food Plan structure.
Contribution, food items, timing, pricing and fulfilment rules: confirm with staff.
Cash-out, substitution, withdrawal or refund rules: confirm before enrolment.
notice: Do not rely on old cards or informal descriptions. Ask staff for the current written structure, included items, fulfilment timing and withdrawal or refund conditions before contributing.
faqs: Is Food Vault currently the Onwa Dezemba Food Plan?
The approved repository source does not confirm that structure, so this page does not present it as a fact.
Which foods are included?
No approved item list is available in the repository. Staff must confirm the current plan.
Will I receive food or cash?
The repository does not contain an approved fulfilment rule. Confirm this before enrolment.
approvalTodos: Whether Onwa Dezemba is the approved current structure
Pricing, substitution, withdrawal and refund rules
